content.problemStatement:
Ben Fero is a popular guy who likes inventing and solving interesting problems. Last weekend, he found a challenging problem, but he could not solve it on his own, so he decided to ask you for help. Can you help Ben Fero solve this problem?

You are given three integers AAA, BBB and CCC. Let's call a triple of integers (a,b,c)(a, b, c)(a,b,c) interesting if it satisfies the following criteria:


1≤a≤A1 \le a \le A1≤a≤A, 1≤b≤B1 \le b \le B1≤b≤B and 1≤c≤C1 \le c \le C1≤c≤C
for any two real numbers xxx and yyy such that x≠0x \neq 0x=0 and y≠0y \neq 0y=0, ax2+2bxy+cy2>x2+y2ax^2 + 2bxy + cy^2 \gt x^2 + y^2ax2+2bxy+cy2>x2+y2 holds
You should find the number of interesting triples. Since this number could be very large, compute it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains three space-separated integers AAA, BBB and CCC.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of interesting triples modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
